ItacPersonItem.Notes: Liceti was professor of philosophy in the Universites of Pisa (since 1600) and Padua (1609-38). In Padua he also taught theoretical medicine (1645-57). He engaged in polemical exchanges with Galilei, in particular on the problem of moon light. It is in this context that Galileo wrote `Lettera al principe Leopoldo di Toscana`. Liceti was `consigliere` and `censore alle stampe` in the Ricovrati Academy, which he joined in 1619. See his biography by Giuseppe Ongaro, in DBI.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
